title: Run HB-MNL NUTS sampling from a pre-built Stan JSON data file
summary: Run Hierarchical Bayes MNL conjoint sampling via the CmdStan NUTS sampler. Accepts a pre-built Stan JSON data file and writes one CmdStan CSV per chain to run_dir. Use fit instead if you want automatic CSV-to-Stan-JSON conversion. Follow with finalize to compute diagnostics and part-worth exports.
description: Low-level sampling entry point that invokes CmdStan directly on a pre-built Stan JSON data file. All Stan model parameters (design matrix, respondent indicators, task indicators, choice indicators, prior hyperparameters) must already be present in the data file. Use `fit` instead for the higher-level workflow that converts a coded-long CSV automatically. `sample` is useful when you need to experiment with different prior settings or data subsets without redoing the CSV conversion step.
usage:
[["hb-stan::sample"]]
data = "<stan_data.json>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "stan_run"
# optional:
num_samples = 1000
num_warmup = 500
num_chains = 4
seed = <int>
thin = <int>
parallel_chains = true

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Path to the Stan JSON data file. Must contain all fields required by the HB-MNL model: design matrix (x_matrix, N×P), respondent indicators (unit_indicator), task indicators (task_indicator), chosen-row indices (positive_util_rows, positive_util_wgt), prior hyperparameters (prior_lkj_eta, sigma_scale, ridge_scale), and per-unit index arrays (row_start, row_len, pos_start, pos_len). The stan_data.json written by `fit` always satisfies these requirements.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. Chain CSV files are written as hb_fit_chain_1.csv, hb_fit_chain_2.csv, etc. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: num_samples
arguments.description: Post-warmup draws per chain. Total posterior draws = num_samples × num_chains. Default 1000.

arguments.name: num_warmup
arguments.description: Warmup (adaptation) iterations per chain. NUTS tunes its step size and mass matrix during warmup; these draws are discarded. Default 500.

arguments.name: num_chains
arguments.description: Number of independent chains. At least 4 is recommended for reliable Rhat and ESS diagnostics. Default 4.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: RNG seed. Each chain receives seed + (chain_index − 1) for independence with reproducibility. Omit for a non-deterministic seed.

arguments.name: thin
arguments.description: Thinning factor: keep every Nth draw. Reduces output file size at the cost of effective sample size. Omit to keep all draws.

arguments.name: parallel_chains
arguments.description: Run chains in parallel using available CPU threads up to num_chains. Set false for serial execution to reduce peak memory. Default true.

details:
`sample` is the low-level CmdStan invocation layer. Unlike `fit`, it does not perform any data conversion — it expects a fully-prepared Stan JSON file and directly invokes the CmdStan NUTS sampler. This is the right command when you already have stan_data.json (from a previous `fit` call) and want to re-run sampling with different sampler settings (different seed, more warmup, thinning) without redoing the conversion.

The NUTS sampler adapts its step size and Euclidean metric during warmup (the dual-averaging / windowed adaptation scheme) and then draws independent samples during the post-warmup phase. With 4 chains you can diagnose convergence via Rhat and ESS in `finalize`.

For the common workflow where you start from a CSV, use `fit` instead — it calls this command internally after converting the CSV.
value:
On success, run_dir contains:

- hb_fit_chain_1.csv, hb_fit_chain_2.csv, ... — one CmdStan CSV file per chain, containing the raw posterior draws.

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, periodic event.run.progress events as chains complete, and a final event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: The stan_data.json file must contain all required fields — use `fit` to generate it from a CSV if you don't have it already.
CmdStan must be installed and on PATH.
Follow with finalize to compute Rhat, ESS, posterior summaries, and part-worths.

see_also.description: Higher-level entry point that converts a coded-long CSV to Stan JSON and then calls sample.
Compute diagnostics and part-worth exports from the chain CSVs written here.
Convenience wrapper: CSV → sampling → finalization in one call.

examples.title: Re-sample from an existing stan_data.json with a different seed
Longer sampling run with extra warmup
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::sample"]]
data = "./runs/hb-stan/stan_data.json"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-long"
run_name = "stan_run2"
num_samples = 2000
num_warmup = 1000
seed = 42

examples.title: Serial chains with thinning to reduce output size
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::sample"]]
data = "./runs/hb-stan/stan_data.json"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-thin"
run_name = "stan_run3"
num_chains = 4
thin = 2
parallel_chains = false

fields.name: data
fields.description: Path to the Stan JSON data file.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for chain CSV files.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: thin
fields.description: Keep every Nth draw. Omit to keep all.

fields.name: parallel_chains
fields.description: Run chains in parallel. Default: true.

artifacts.title: Raw posterior chain draws
artifacts.description: One CmdStan CSV file per chain with all post-warmup draws from the NUTS sampler. Pass the run_dir to finalize to produce convergence diagnostics, posterior summaries, and the part-worth matrix.
